summary: Decent show with standout moments like Comes a Time banter, West L.A. Fadeaway and strong drums/space; recording quality is unmentioned but appears listenable.
review: The reviews highlight several musical high points – Jerry’s in‑flight commentary teaching Vince on “Comes a Time,” solid renditions of West L.A. Fadeaway, Cumberland Blues, Samson & Delilah and Standing On The Moon, plus an enjoyable Drums/Space stretch. Recording details are sparse, but no complaints suggest the source is at least reasonably clear.

ai_rating.rationale: Show receives praise for specific songs and banter but also notes it isn’t among the band’s best; recording quality is not discussed, leading to an overall solid but not exceptional rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewer mentions sound issues or clarity problems, implying a listenable source despite lack of explicit details.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Mix of sound‑check material and live performance; banter and teaching moments add character, though some feel it’s not the band’s top tier.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his in‑flight commentary – instructing Vince on “Comes a Time” and telling Bob how to play his own song.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned when Jerry tells him how to play “Easy Answers.”
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince receives guidance from Jerry during the performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums/Space segment described as cool and enjoyable.
